text: {"claims":[{"statement":"The guidance for babies at higher risk of food allergy (i.e., those with eczema or other known food allergies) is to introduce cooked eggs into the diet from around 4 months of age and maintain intake.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The guidance for babies at higher risk of food allergy (i.e., those with eczema or other known food allergies) is to introduce cooked eggs into the diet from around 4 months of age and maintain intake","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states the recommended timing and continuation of cooked egg introduction for high‑risk infants.","population":"babies at higher risk of food allergy","intervention":"introduce cooked eggs from around 4 months of age and maintain intake","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Human cases of the food‑borne illness caused by salmonella steadily rose beginning in the 1980s and were determined to be caused by consumption of eggs from chickens infected with pathogenic Salmonella serovars.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Beginning in the 1980s, human cases of the food-borne illness caused by salmonella steadily rose and were determined to be caused by consumption of eggs from chickens infected with pathogenic _Salmonella_ serovars","entailment":"The sentence reports a temporal increase in salmonella cases and attributes the cause to eating contaminated eggs, providing a causal link.","population":"human cases","intervention":"consumption of eggs from chickens infected with pathogenic Salmonella serovars","comparator":"","outcome":"salmonella illness","timeframe":"beginning in the 1980s","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Just two years after implementation of the Lion Code of Practice, human cases of salmonella dramatically reduced.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Just two years after implementation of the Lion Code of Practice, human cases of salmonella dramatically reduced","entailment":"The quoted statement indicates a temporal association between the Lion Code implementation and a sharp decline in salmonella cases.","population":"human cases of salmonella","intervention":"implementation of the Lion Code of Practice","comparator":"","outcome":"salmonella cases reduced dramatically","timeframe":"two years after implementation","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"More than 90% of all UK eggs are produced to the Lion Code of Practice and bear the British Lion mark.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"More than 90% of all UK eggs are produced to the Lion Code of Practice and bear the British Lion mark","entailment":"The sentence provides a factual proportion indicating the extent of Lion Code compliance among UK eggs.","population":"all UK eggs","intervention":"produced to the Lion Code of Practice","comparator":"","outcome":"bear the British Lion mark","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"More than 90%","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"In 2017, the Food Standards Agency updated their guidance to expand on the groups that could safely consume raw or lightly cooked eggs, provided they bear the Lion Quality mark, including infants, children, pregnant women and the elderly.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"In 2017, the Food Standards Agency updated their guidance to expand on the groups that could safely consume raw or lightly cooked eggs, provided they bear the Lion Quality mark, including infants, children, pregnant women and the elderly","entailment":"The quoted passage states the 2017 policy change and lists the population groups now permitted to eat raw or lightly cooked eggs with the Lion Quality mark.","population":"infants, children, pregnant women and the elderly","intervention":"consume raw or lightly cooked eggs that bear the Lion Quality mark","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"2017","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"For general populations, SRMA of PCS found neutral or beneficial effects of moderate egg consumption on CVD mortality and risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For general populations, SRMA of PCS found neutral or beneficial effects of moderate egg consumption on CVD mortality and risk","entailment":"The sentence reports the result of a systematic review/meta‑analysis indicating that moderate egg intake is associated with neutral or positive outcomes for cardiovascular disease mortality and risk.","population":"general populations","intervention":"moderate egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"CVD mortality and risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"moderate","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Some SRMA of RCT found modest increases in serum lipid levels which may be due to the high intakes of eggs given during these short‑to‑medium term interventions which do not reflect mean habitual intakes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Some SRMA of RCT found modest increases in serum lipid levels which may be due to the high intakes of eggs given during these short-to-medium term interventions which do not reflect mean habitual intakes","entailment":"The quoted text describes an observed modest rise in serum lipids in trial settings, attributing it to unusually high egg intakes over short‑to‑medium durations.","population":"","intervention":"high intakes of eggs in short‑to‑medium term interventions","comparator":"","outcome":"serum lipid levels modestly increased","timeframe":"short-to-medium term interventions","quantifiers":"modest","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"HDL‑c levels typically increased during these studies which can rebalance lipoprotein profile and, in terms of disease risk, may mitigate some of the negative effects of higher TC or LDL‑c.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"HDL-c levels typically increased during these studies which can rebalance lipoprotein profile and, in terms of disease risk, may mitigate some of the negative effects of higher TC or LDL-c","entailment":"The sentence reports that HDL‑c rose in the studies and suggests this change could offset adverse impacts of elevated total or LDL cholesterol.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption in the studies","comparator":"","outcome":"HDL‑c levels increased, potential mitigation of TC/LDL‑c effects","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"typically","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"SRMA of PCS reported an increased risk of developing T2D when participants ate eggs more regularly.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"SRMA of PCS reported an increased risk of developing T2D when participants ate eggs more regularly","entailment":"The quoted systematic review/meta‑analysis indicates a positive association between more frequent egg consumption and higher type‑2 diabetes risk.","population":"","intervention":"more regular egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"increased risk of developing T2D","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"In contrast, RCT in participants with MetS reported neutral or positive effects on disease markers including insulin resistance and serum lipids.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In contrast, RCT in participants with MetS—arguably at higher risk of T2D and CVD than the general population—reported neutral or positive effects on disease markers including insulin resistance and serum lipids","entailment":"The sentence conveys that a randomized trial among MetS participants found no worsening and possibly beneficial changes in insulin resistance and lipid profiles.","population":"participants with MetS","intervention":"egg consumption in the RCT","comparator":"","outcome":"neutral or positive effects on insulin resistance and serum lipids","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Observational studies are not designed to determine causality; hence, confounding is a problem.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"A major limitation of observational studies is that they are not designed to determine causality; hence, confounding is a problem","entailment":"The quoted sentence explains a methodological limitation of observational research, namely its inability to establish causal relationships due to confounding.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs represent only a tiny proportion of daily energy intake (2% in the UK NDNS on average).","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs represent only a tiny proportion of daily energy intake (2% in the UK NDNS on average)","entailment":"The quoted figure quantifies the contribution of eggs to total daily energy, indicating it is about 2% on average in the UK survey.","population":"UK population (NDNS)","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"daily energy intake proportion","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"2%","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Consuming one egg a day accounted for <1% of CHD risk compared with 40% represented by all modifiable lifestyle risk factors.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"consuming one egg a day accounted for <1% of CHD risk compared with 40% represented by all modifiable lifestyle risk factors","entailment":"The sentence provides a risk apportionment estimate showing that a single daily egg contributes less than 1% to coronary heart disease risk, far less than lifestyle factors.","population":"","intervention":"consuming one egg a day","comparator":"all modifiable lifestyle risk factors","outcome":"CHD risk contribution","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"<1%","modality":"associational"}]}
